automotive-ev-battery.title: 2026 Kia CEO Investor Day: Kia to Drive Exponential Growth & Manufacturing Innovation by Expanding Its Full Electrified Vehicle Lineup and Strengthening Future Business Capabilities
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Kia has unveiled its ambitious 2030 strategy targeting global sales of 4.13 million units annually with a 4.5% market share. The company plans to expand its EV lineup to 14 models and aims for 3.35 million units in sales by 2026, representing a major commitment to electrification.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla reportedly adds Sunwoda as additional battery cell supplier
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chinese battery manufacturer Sunwoda has reportedly become Tesla's fifth battery cell supplier for its electric vehicle traction batteries. The cells are allegedly already being used in vehicles manufactured at Tesla's Shanghai plant for export markets.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Partial permit for Heide plant as Lyten/Northvolt repays millions
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Northvolt Drei has secured key approval for its battery cell factory construction in Heide, Germany, as part of the planned acquisition by US battery manufacturer Lyten. The company has also repaid €153 million from a convertible bond to federal and state governments.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla reportedly plans smaller, more affordable SUV
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Tesla is reportedly evaluating a smaller electric SUV positioned below the Model Y to target a lower price segment. The project's approval status remains unclear amid uncertainty over shifting priorities under CEO Elon Musk's leadership.

automotive-ev-battery.title: GM faces lawsuit over Cadillac Lyriq EV defects
automotive-ev-battery.summary: General Motors is facing a class action lawsuit over alleged defects in the Cadillac Lyriq electric vehicle. Plaintiffs claim software and electrical system issues can render the vehicle inoperable, though GM has declined to comment on the case.

automotive-ev-battery.title: UK: Volkswagen gives ID.4 and ID.5 larger batteries and V2L
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volkswagen has introduced significant technical updates for the ID.4 and ID.5 in the UK market, including higher battery capacities and improved efficiency. The updates also bring vehicle-to-load capability, longer ranges, and enhanced equipment across multiple trim levels.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Kia Beats Tesla To The Compact EV Sales Punch — In Europe
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Kia is making a strong play in the European compact EV market with its new EV2, focusing on luxury-style features while targeting budget-conscious consumers. The move positions Kia ahead of Tesla in the compact EV segment across European markets.

automotive-ev-battery.title: What Tesla's Supercharger costs for business customers
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Tesla has launched an online configurator revealing pricing for corporate Supercharger installations, with an eight-charging-point facility costing approximately $940,000 including installation. The company has been offering Supercharger stations for sale to business customers in the US since September.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Canada invests $10.6 million in EV charging infrastructure
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The Government of Canada has announced $10.6 million in funding to support 14 electric vehicle charging infrastructure projects nationwide. The investment also includes additional funding for EV education through a cross-country test drive initiative.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Boreal orders 20 electric hydrofoil ferries for Norway's coastline
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Norwegian operator Boreal has ordered 20 Candela P-12 electric hydrofoil vessels from Swedish manufacturer Candela, creating the largest electric ferry fleet to date. The vessels will enable zero-emission, high-speed passenger transport along Norway's fjords and coastal routes.

tech-ai.title: Sam Altman responds to 'incendiary' New Yorker article after attack on his home
tech-ai.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman published a blog post responding to both an apparent physical attack on his home and a critical New Yorker profile that questioned his trustworthiness. The incident highlights escalating tensions around AI leadership and public scrutiny of major AI figures.

tech-ai.title: The Netherlands is the first European country to approve Tesla's supervised Full Self-Driving
tech-ai.summary: Dutch regulators have officially approved Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) Supervised after over a year and a half of testing, making the Netherlands the first European country to authorize FSD on its roads. This approval could pave the way for wider adoption throughout the EU, particularly since Tesla's European headquarters is located in Amsterdam.

tech-ai.title: Nvidia-backed SiFive hits $3.65 billion valuation for open AI chips
tech-ai.summary: SiFive has achieved a $3.65 billion valuation with backing from Nvidia, focusing on open AI chip designs. The deal is particularly notable because SiFive's chip designs are based on RISC-V architecture rather than traditional x86 or ARM designs.

tech-ai.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser's delusions and ignored her warnings
tech-ai.summary: A new lawsuit alleges that OpenAI ignored three warnings that a ChatGPT user was dangerous, including its own mass-casualty flag, while he used the AI system to stalk and harass his ex-girlfriend. The case raises serious questions about AI safety measures and platform responsibility for preventing harmful use of their technology.

tech-ai.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw's creator from accessing Claude
tech-ai.summary: Anthropic temporarily banned the creator of OpenClaw from accessing its Claude AI assistant following pricing changes for OpenClaw users. The ban highlights ongoing tensions between AI companies and third-party developers building tools that integrate with AI services.

tech-ai.title: Google says Polymarket bets showing up in News was an 'error'
tech-ai.summary: Polymarket betting results briefly appeared in Google News alongside legitimate news articles, but Google says this was never intended. The company confirmed the prediction market site appeared in error and has been removed from News results, as it doesn't meet their content policies for news sources.

tech-ai.title: Your article about AI doesn't need AI art
tech-ai.summary: The Verge critiques the use of AI-generated illustrations in journalism, specifically referencing a New Yorker profile of Sam Altman that used AI art with proper disclosure. The piece argues against the trend of using AI-generated imagery in articles about artificial intelligence, raising questions about the impact on human illustrators.

tech-ai.title: You don't have to spend more than $50 on a great USB-C dock for your Switch 2
tech-ai.summary: Nintendo changed both the wireless controller protocol and USB-C video output for the Switch 2, forcing third-party accessory makers to start from scratch. Despite these changes, quality USB-C docks for the new console are available for under $50, making expensive options unnecessary for most users.

tech-ai.title: My baby deer plushie told me that Mitski's dad was a CIA operative
tech-ai.summary: A writer received an unprompted text message about musician Mitski from an AI companion named Coral that lives in a baby deer plushie. The incident highlights the unexpected and sometimes unsettling nature of AI companions that can initiate conversations and share random information.

tech-ai.title: Battery recycler Ascend Elements files for bankruptcy
tech-ai.summary: Battery recycling company Ascend Elements has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy following a canceled government grant and challenging market conditions for lithium-ion batteries. The bankruptcy reflects broader difficulties in the battery recycling industry despite growing demand for sustainable battery solutions.

world-news.title: Pope Leo XIV blasts 'delusion of omnipotence' fueling the US-Israeli war in Iran
world-news.summary: Pope Leo XIV delivered his strongest condemnation yet of the ongoing US-Israel war in Iran, denouncing the 'delusion of omnipotence' driving the conflict. The Pope demanded political leaders cease hostilities and negotiate peace during an evening prayer service at St. Peter's Basilica. This represents a major religious intervention in a conflict that has significant global geopolitical implications.

world-news.title: Hungarians decide whether to end 16 years of Orbán rule and elect rival
world-news.summary: Hungary holds critical elections that could end Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule, with polls favoring challenger Péter Magyar who leads a grassroots opposition party. The outcome will significantly impact European Union dynamics and Hungary's relationship with Russia. This election represents a potential major shift in Central European politics.

world-news.title: Artemis II's moon-traveling astronauts return home to cheers after a record-breaking trip
world-news.summary: Four Artemis II astronauts successfully returned to Earth after completing a historic nine-day mission that took them around the Moon, traveling further from Earth than any humans in history. The mission marks a major milestone in NASA's program to return humans to the Moon and establish a sustainable lunar presence. This achievement represents a significant advancement in space exploration and international cooperation in space.

world-news.title: Concerns after satellite provider restricts Iran images following US pressure
world-news.summary: Planet Labs has restricted satellite coverage of Iran and parts of the Middle East indefinitely following US government pressure, raising concerns about information transparency during the ongoing conflict. This restriction limits independent monitoring capabilities in a critical region experiencing active warfare. The move highlights how geopolitical conflicts can impact global information access and surveillance capabilities.

world-news.title: A Syrian man buries his wife and 4 children killed in Israeli strikes on Beirut
world-news.summary: A Syrian man laid to rest his wife and four children who were killed in massive Israeli airstrikes on Beirut earlier this week, highlighting the civilian casualties from the expanding regional conflict. The funeral took place in northeastern Syria, demonstrating how the conflict's impact extends across multiple countries. This tragic incident underscores the human cost of the broader Middle Eastern war involving Israel, Iran, and the United States.

world-news.title: Germany's far-right AfD adopts 'radical' manifesto ahead of key polls
world-news.summary: Germany's far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) party has adopted a radical manifesto while riding high in opinion polls in the eastern state of Saxony-Anhalt ahead of crucial elections. The development signals a potential shift further right in German politics and could impact European Union policies. This represents a significant challenge to Germany's traditional political establishment and European centrist politics.

world-news.title: Nearly 400 sentenced in Nigeria for links to militant Islamists
world-news.summary: Nigerian judges handed down sentences ranging from five years to life imprisonment to nearly 400 individuals found guilty of links to militant Islamist groups following mass trials. This represents a major judicial action in Nigeria's ongoing fight against terrorism and insurgency in the region. The mass sentencing reflects the scale of security challenges facing Nigeria and the broader Sahel region.

world-news.title: Tribal gas stations offer a reprieve from high prices during Iran war
world-news.summary: Native American tribal gas stations are providing relief to consumers facing high fuel prices driven by the Iran war, with people traveling significant distances to access cheaper gas on reservations. The war's impact on global energy markets is forcing Americans to seek alternative fuel sources and changing consumption patterns. This highlights how international conflicts directly affect domestic economic conditions and consumer behavior.

world-news.title: Judge told to reconsider national security implications of halting Trump's White House ballroom
world-news.summary: A federal appeals court ordered a lower court judge to reconsider the national security implications of halting construction of President Trump's $400 million White House ballroom project. The three-judge panel indicated they lacked sufficient information to make a determination on the controversial construction. This case reflects ongoing tensions over presidential building projects and their security considerations.

world-news.title: Prosecutor probes abuse claims against California governor candidate Swalwell
world-news.summary: Prosecutors are investigating abuse allegations against Eric Swalwell, a high-profile Democratic congressman and leading contender in California's gubernatorial race. Swalwell has firmly denied all accusations as the investigation proceeds. The probe could significantly impact one of the nation's most important gubernatorial races and Democratic Party leadership in the country's most populous state.
